ats.mermaid:
%%{init:{'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'fontFamily':'Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif','fontSize':'14px','primaryColor':'#eef2f7','primaryBorderColor':'#1A2238','primaryTextColor':'#1A2238','lineColor':'#54617a'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  __start__((start)):::term
  harvest["harvest"]:::det
  map["map"]:::llm
  readiness{{"readiness"}}:::det
  score["score"]:::llm
  shortlist["shortlist"]:::det
  briefing["briefing"]:::det
  __end__((end)):::term
  __start__ --> harvest
  harvest --> map
  map --> readiness
  readiness --> score
  score --> shortlist
  shortlist --> briefing
  briefing --> __end__
  classDef llm fill:#e8e7fb,stroke:#5b57c7,color:#2a2870;
  classDef vis fill:#dcefef,stroke:#2a8f8f,color:#124d4d;
  classDef det fill:#e6ebf3,stroke:#5b6b86,color:#2b3750;
  classDef term fill:#1A2238,stroke:#1A2238,color:#ffffff;
ats.note: Deterministic commissioning orchestrator (not a LangGraph StateGraph). The LLM is touched only twice: mapper._classify (free-text trigger → persona/models) and score._llm_judge (one relevance vote). "Trigger proposes, human disposes."

graphs.mermaid:
%%{init:{'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'fontFamily':'Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif','fontSize':'14px','primaryColor':'#eef2f7','primaryBorderColor':'#1A2238','primaryTextColor':'#1A2238','lineColor':'#54617a'}}}%%
flowchart TB
  __start__((start)):::term
  material["material"]:::det
  brief["brief"]:::det
  plan["plan"]:::llm
  draft["draft"]:::llm
  reconcile_charts["reconcile_charts"]:::det
  illustrate["illustrate"]:::vis
  reconcile_dashboard["reconcile_dashboard"]:::det
  assemble["assemble"]:::det
  __end__((end)):::term
  __start__ --> material
  assemble --> __end__
  brief --> plan
  draft --> reconcile_charts
  illustrate --> reconcile_dashboard
  material --> brief
  plan --> draft
  reconcile_charts --> illustrate
  reconcile_dashboard --> assemble
  classDef llm fill:#e8e7fb,stroke:#5b57c7,color:#2a2870;
  classDef vis fill:#dcefef,stroke:#2a8f8f,color:#124d4d;
  classDef det fill:#e6ebf3,stroke:#5b6b86,color:#2b3750;
  classDef term fill:#1A2238,stroke:#1A2238,color:#ffffff;
graphs.name: article_graph

graphs.mermaid:
%%{init:{'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'fontFamily':'Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif','fontSize':'14px','primaryColor':'#eef2f7','primaryBorderColor':'#1A2238','primaryTextColor':'#1A2238','lineColor':'#54617a'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  __start__((start)):::term
  library["library"]:::det
  propose["propose"]:::llm
  validate{{"validate"}}:::det
  __end__((end)):::term
  __start__ --> library
  library --> propose
  propose --> validate
  validate -.-> __end__
  validate -.-> propose
  classDef llm fill:#e8e7fb,stroke:#5b57c7,color:#2a2870;
  classDef vis fill:#dcefef,stroke:#2a8f8f,color:#124d4d;
  classDef det fill:#e6ebf3,stroke:#5b6b86,color:#2b3750;
  classDef term fill:#1A2238,stroke:#1A2238,color:#ffffff;
graphs.name: selector

graphs.mermaid:
%%{init:{'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'fontFamily':'Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif','fontSize':'14px','primaryColor':'#eef2f7','primaryBorderColor':'#1A2238','primaryTextColor':'#1A2238','lineColor':'#54617a'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  __start__((start)):::term
  framer["framer"]:::llm
  proposer["proposer"]:::llm
  critic_panel["critic_panel"]:::llm
  compile["compile"]:::det
  critique{{"critique"}}:::vis
  reviser["reviser"]:::llm
  judge{{"judge"}}:::vis
  __end__((end)):::term
  __start__ --> framer
  compile --> critique
  critic_panel --> compile
  critique -.-> judge
  critique -.-> reviser
  framer --> proposer
  judge -.-> __end__
  judge -.-> proposer
  proposer --> critic_panel
  reviser --> compile
  classDef llm fill:#e8e7fb,stroke:#5b57c7,color:#2a2870;
  classDef vis fill:#dcefef,stroke:#2a8f8f,color:#124d4d;
  classDef det fill:#e6ebf3,stroke:#5b6b86,color:#2b3750;
  classDef term fill:#1A2238,stroke:#1A2238,color:#ffffff;
graphs.name: studio

graphs.mermaid:
%%{init:{'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'fontFamily':'Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif','fontSize':'14px','primaryColor':'#eef2f7','primaryBorderColor':'#1A2238','primaryTextColor':'#1A2238','lineColor':'#54617a'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  __start__((start)):::term
  extract["extract"]:::llm
  adjudicate{{"adjudicate"}}:::det
  verdict["verdict"]:::det
  __end__((end)):::term
  __start__ --> extract
  adjudicate -.-> extract
  adjudicate -.-> verdict
  extract --> adjudicate
  verdict --> __end__
  classDef llm fill:#e8e7fb,stroke:#5b57c7,color:#2a2870;
  classDef vis fill:#dcefef,stroke:#2a8f8f,color:#124d4d;
  classDef det fill:#e6ebf3,stroke:#5b6b86,color:#2b3750;
  classDef term fill:#1A2238,stroke:#1A2238,color:#ffffff;
graphs.name: judge

fields.desc: The single communicative job, one sentence (compare/trend/spread/relationship/rank/part-to-whole).
fields.name: message

fields.desc: Provenance line, e.g. model + papers + as-of date.
fields.name: source_note

fields.desc: Why this form/encoding carries the insight better than the alternatives.
fields.name: rationale

fields.desc: Long-form rows; every value from an executed model output — the LLM never authors a number.
fields.name: data

fields.desc: The ONE thing the chart must say, one sentence, naming the communicative job (compare/trend/spread/relationship/part-to-whole/rank/divergence).
fields.name: message

fields.desc: 2–3 chart FORMS worth proposing for this message+data-shape, from: line, area, bar, grouped_bar, dumbbell, slope, point, connected_scatter, bubble, heatmap, ridgeline, waterfall. Ordered best-first. Avoid defaulting to line/bar unless the data genuinely calls for it.
fields.name: candidate_marks

fields.desc: Why these forms fit the message and the number/shape of variables.
fields.name: reasoning

fields.desc: True = ships (expressive, effective, carries the insight, and is differentiated — not something a muppet with the FT could reproduce).
fields.name: verdict

fields.desc: One or two sentences justifying the verdict against those criteria.
fields.name: notes

fields.desc: Consensus of the three experts: why this encoding carries the insight best (effectiveness + insight-carriage + differentiation vs a vanilla default).
fields.name: rationale

fields.desc: True if the rendered chart is clean and reads clearly with no defects worth fixing. If omitted, inferred from whether defects were listed.
fields.name: ok

fields.desc: Concrete rendered-pixel problems: label collisions, occlusion, clipped/overshooting axes, unreadable spaghetti, a legend that duplicates direct labels, a near-empty panel, etc.
fields.name: defects

fields.desc: Specific, encoding-level changes to fix each defect (e.g. 'set y scale.domain to [-1,6]', 'drop the legend, keep direct labels', 'switch color_job to diverging').
fields.name: fixes
